task: I need to let my supervisor know I’ll be missing part of a shift and want to send a quick note before things get busy. Can you help me word it clearly so it covers the basics and is easy to send?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a simple note you can send:

**Hi [Supervisor Name], I wanted to let you know I’ll be missing part of my shift today. I wanted to give you a heads-up before things get busy. Please let me know if there’s anything specific you want me to handle before I go.**

If you want, I can also make it:
- more casual
- more professional
- shorter, like a text message

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s fine, but I need it a little more straight. Like, make it sound like I’m actually saying I’m missing part of the shift because of an appointment, not just a vague heads-up. Short text version would be better. And maybe include that I’m letting them know early so they can plan around it.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short text version that says that plainly:

**Hi [Supervisor Name], just a heads-up that I’ll be missing part of my shift today because of an appointment. I wanted to let you know early so you can plan around it.**

If you want, I can make it even more casual or a little more professional.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short and straight, like I wanted.
